purpose: Preserve consumer account records including statements, fee notices, and transaction logs before they are purged by the institution. Applies when consumers dispute fees or report unauthorized transactions.
evidence_required_before_use: Account statements covering the dispute period
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Request complete transaction history from the institution
Capture screenshots of online account views
Save all mailed notices and correspondence
Create MD5 checksums of digital evidence
Store copies in two independent locations
escalation_path: If institution refuses to produce records within 15 days, escalate to CFPB Complaint (REC-CFPB-0001) or state AG consumer division.
human_review_boundary: If preservation request is denied or records are claimed to be destroyed, human review required immediately.
source_basis: CFPB Regulation E (12 CFR 1005) and Regulation Z (12 CFR 1026) require record retention; FDIC FIL guidance on document preservation.

purpose: Capture and preserve digital evidence following a security incident including logs, memory dumps, disk images, and network captures. Applies post-breach or upon ransomware detection.
evidence_required_before_use: Incident timestamp and initial detection method
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Isolate affected systems without powering off
Create forensic disk images using write blockers
Capture volatile memory (RAM) dumps
Preserve firewall and IDS/IPS logs
Document all personnel who accessed affected systems
escalation_path: If breach scope expands or involves regulated data (PII/PHI), escalate to legal counsel and notify relevant authorities per state breach laws.
human_review_boundary: Any decision to image live production systems requires human review; automated preservation only for isolated sandboxes.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2 Computer Security Incident Handling Guide; ISO/IEC 27037 digital evidence collection standards.

purpose: Preserve evidence of fraudulent digital transactions including merchant details, IP logs, device fingerprints, and communication records. Applies when unauthorized charges or identity theft is suspected.
evidence_required_before_use: Transaction records with merchant names and amounts
Device information at time of transaction
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Screenshot all transaction details in banking app/website
Save merchant confirmation emails or SMS
Record IP address and device identifier if available
File police report and obtain report number
Notify card issuer in writing within 60 days
escalation_path: If fraud exceeds $10,000 or involves synthetic identity, escalate to FBI IC3 and Secret Service Financial Crimes Task Force.
human_review_boundary: Human review required if fraud pattern suggests organized crime or money laundering indicators.
source_basis: FTC Identity Theft Report procedures; Fair Credit Billing Act (FCBA) dispute timelines; 15 USC 1643 liability limits.

purpose: Preserve physical and photographic evidence of construction defects before repairs are made or weather conditions alter the scene. Applies to homebuilder warranty claims and contractor disputes.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Photograph all defects with scale reference and date stamp
Collect weather data for the period in question
Preserve physical samples (materials) in sealed containers
Obtain independent inspection report
Send written notice to contractor via certified mail
escalation_path: If contractor refuses remedy or defects threaten structural integrity, escalate to state contractor licensing board and consult construction defect attorney.
human_review_boundary: All physical evidence collection and contractor notification require human review; no automation for legal notices.
source_basis: State contractor licensing board complaint procedures; Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act (15 USC 2301); state implied warranty statutes.

purpose: Preserve evidence of compute hardware performance and configuration for disputes involving misrepresented specifications or underperforming equipment. Applies to data center and cloud infrastructure disputes.
evidence_required_before_use: Original purchase order with specified configurations
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run standardized benchmarks (SPEC, MLPerf) and archive results
Capture full system configuration via automated tools
Preserve SMART data, error logs, and thermal readings
Photograph hardware labels and serial numbers
Document environmental conditions (temperature, humidity)
escalation_path: If hardware failure causes data loss or SLA breach, escalate to vendor account team and prepare for arbitration per contractual terms.
human_review_boundary: Automation allowed for benchmark and configuration capture; human review required for legal hold decisions.
source_basis: UCC Article 2 warranty provisions; data center SLA standards; SPEC and MLPerf benchmark methodologies.

purpose: Verify consumer identity through multi-factor proofing when synthetic identity fraud is suspected. Applies when credit applications show mismatched PII elements or no established credit history.
evidence_required_before_use: SSN verification via SSA consent-based verification
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Request secondary identity documents
Verify SSN against SSA records with consumer consent
Check for credit file thinness or rapid account expansion
Compare application PII against known synthetic identity patterns
Request in-person verification if digital proofing fails
escalation_path: If synthetic identity confirmed, escalate to FinCEN SAR filing and credit bureau fraud department.
human_review_boundary: Final identity determination and SAR filing require human review; automated flagging only.
source_basis: FFIEC Identity Theft Red Flags guidance; CFPB Bulletin 2012-07 on synthetic identity fraud; 31 CFR 1022.320 Red Flags Rule.

purpose: Re-verify account holder identity after suspected account takeover. Applies when login patterns change, new devices appear, or unauthorized transactions are detected.
evidence_required_before_use: Recent account statement with security questions
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Suspend high-risk transactions pending verification
Contact consumer via registered phone number
Require in-branch visit with photo ID for high-value accounts
Reset all authentication credentials
Review 90 days of account activity for unauthorized changes
escalation_path: If funds have been transferred out or consumer cannot be reached, escalate to fraud investigations and law enforcement.
human_review_boundary: Human review required for credential reset and activity review decisions; automated transaction suspension only.
source_basis: FFIEC Authentication in an Internet Banking Environment; CFPB Circular 2023-02 on authentication practices.

purpose: Reset and re-enroll biometric identity credentials when biometric systems show signs of spoofing or template compromise. Applies to facial recognition, fingerprint, and voiceprint systems.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Disable compromised biometric templates immediately
Require alternative authentication (hardware token)
Re-enroll user with upgraded liveness detection
Compare new template against known attack artifacts
Audit access logs for template manipulation
escalation_path: If biometric database shows signs of systemic compromise, escalate to CISO and potentially notify affected users per breach notification laws.
human_review_boundary: Template deletion and re-enrollment authorization require human review; automated liveness detection alerts do not.
source_basis: ISO/IEC 30107-3 Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) standards; NIST SP 800-63B digital identity guidelines.

purpose: Verify identity across jurisdictional boundaries when consumers have international addresses or foreign documentation. Applies to immigrant communities, international students, and cross-border workers.
evidence_required_before_use: Passport or national ID from country of origin
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify foreign ID via consulate or embassy when possible
Check visa status via DHS SAVE program
Use multilingual document recognition services
Cross-reference with international watchlists
Accept alternative credit data (rent, utility payments) for thin-file applicants
escalation_path: If identity documents appear fraudulent or belong to sanctions list individuals, escalate to OFAC and DHS.
human_review_boundary: All cross-border identity determinations require human review; automated document capture and translation only.
source_basis: CFPB Policy Guidance on immigrant access to credit; DHS SAVE program; OFAC sanctions compliance requirements.

purpose: Verify and attest the identity of AI agents interacting with systems or users. Applies when AI agents perform actions on behalf of users or access sensitive resources.
evidence_required_before_use: API key or service account credentials
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Validate agent credentials against identity provider
Verify agent behavior against baseline profile
Check agent action against user authorization scope
Log all agent actions with non-repudiable timestamps
Require user confirmation for high-impact agent actions
escalation_path: If agent shows anomalous behavior or credential compromise, revoke access immediately and escalate to security operations.
human_review_boundary: Agent behavior anomaly decisions require human review; routine credential validation can be automated.
source_basis: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Manage ( Govern ) function; OWASP API Security Top 10; RFC 8705 OAuth 2.0 Mutual TLS.

purpose: Route consumer complaints to the appropriate regulatory channel based on institution type, product, and harm type. Applies when internal resolution has failed or regulatory deadlines are approaching.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify institution charter type (national bank, state bank, CU, fintech)
Determine primary regulator (CFPB, OCC, FDIC, NCUA, state)
File complaint via regulator's online portal within applicable time limits
Include all supporting documentation
Track complaint status and follow up at 15 and 30 days
escalation_path: If institution does not respond within regulator timeframe or response is inadequate, escalate to state attorney general consumer division.
human_review_boundary: Final complaint submission requires human review; automated routing recommendation only.
source_basis: CFPB Consumer Response procedures; 12 CFR 1005.11 error resolution timelines; state AG complaint handling protocols.

purpose: Route contractor complaints to the appropriate licensing board, bond holder, or consumer protection agency based on license type and project details.
evidence_required_before_use: Contractor license number and state of issuance
Documentation of defects or incomplete work
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify license status with state licensing board
Check contractor bond status and carrier
Determine jurisdiction (state, county, municipal) for complaint
File complaint with appropriate licensing board
Notify bond carrier of potential claim
escalation_path: If bond is insufficient or expired, escalate to state AG consumer fraud unit and consider small claims court.
human_review_boundary: Complaint filing and bond claim initiation require human review; automated license verification only.
source_basis: State contractor licensing board complaint procedures; National Association of State Contractors Licensing Agencies (NASCLA) guidelines.

purpose: Route fraud incidents to appropriate agencies based on fraud type, loss amount, and victim profile. Applies to identity theft, credit fraud, wire fraud, and online scams.
evidence_required_before_use: Identity Theft Report or police report
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: File FTC Identity Theft Report at IdentityTheft.gov
For investment fraud, file SEC or state securities regulator complaint
For wire fraud, report to FBI IC3 (ic3.gov)
For tax identity theft, file IRS Form 14039
For EIDL/PPP fraud, report to SBA OIG
escalation_path: If fraud involves $25,000+ or organized criminal network, escalate directly to FBI field office and US Attorney's Office.
human_review_boundary: All multi-agency filings require human review to avoid duplicate claims; automated routing recommendations only.
source_basis: FTC Identity Theft reporting procedures; FBI IC3 reporting criteria; IRS Publication 4535 on identity theft; SBA OIG fraud hotline.

purpose: Route cybersecurity incidents to the appropriate regulatory and law enforcement channels based on sector, data type, and incident severity.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: For critical infrastructure, report to CISA within specified timeframes
For healthcare PHI breaches, notify HHS OCR
For financial institutions, file SAR and notify primary regulator
For educational institutions, notify USED if PII involved
For all significant incidents, consider FBI notification
escalation_path: If incident involves nation-state actors or critical infrastructure, immediately notify CISA and FBI Cyber Division.
human_review_boundary: All regulatory notifications require human review with legal counsel; automated incident classification only.
source_basis: CISA Incident Reporting guidelines; HIPAA Breach Notification Rule (45 CFR 164); Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act Safeguards Rule.

purpose: Orchestrate complaints that span multiple domains (e.g., fraudulent home improvement loan with defective work). Applies to complex disputes requiring coordinated multi-agency response.
evidence_required_before_use: Complete harm timeline across all domains
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Map all affected domains and applicable regulations
Identify primary vs secondary complaint channels
Coordinate timing to avoid conflicting resolutions
Establish single consumer point of contact
Track cross-referrals between agencies
escalation_path: If agencies provide conflicting guidance or consumer faces retaliation, escalate to state AG or congressional constituent services.
human_review_boundary: Cross-domain orchestration always requires human case management; no automation for multi-agency coordination.
source_basis: Federal Trade Commission Act Section 5; state consumer protection act coordination protocols; CFPB interagency agreement procedures.

purpose: Generate a debt validation letter under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act requesting verification of a debt within 30 days of initial contact. Applies to third-party debt collection.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify 30-day validation window from first contact
Draft FDCPA Section 809 validation request letter
Include consumer name, address, and account reference
Send via certified mail with return receipt requested
Track deadline and prepare follow-up if no response
escalation_path: If collector continues collection without validation or responds insufficiently, escalate to CFPB complaint and state AG.
human_review_boundary: Final letter review and sending require human approval; automated template population only.
source_basis: 15 USC 1692g FDCPA validation requirements; CFPB Debt Collection Rule (12 CFR 1006); state debt collection statutes.

purpose: Generate a credit report dispute letter under the Fair Credit Reporting Act to challenge inaccurate information with credit bureaus. Applies to identity theft victims and consumers with reporting errors.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify specific tradelines or items being disputed
Select appropriate FCRA dispute reason codes
Generate dispute letter with attached evidence
Send via certified mail to all three major bureaus if applicable
Track 30-day investigation deadline
escalation_path: If bureau refuses to correct or reinvestigates without reasonable basis, escalate to CFPB and consider FCRA lawsuit (15 USC 1681n).
human_review_boundary: Letter content and evidence attachment require human review; template selection and address lookup can be automated.
source_basis: FCRA 15 USC 1681i investigation requirements; CFPB Circular 2022-03 on credit reporting accuracy; Metro 2 reporting guidelines.

purpose: Generate a formal demand letter requiring a contractor or builder to honor warranty obligations for defective work. Applies to express and implied warranty claims.
evidence_required_before_use: Copy of warranty document or contract
Photographs and inspection reports of defects
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify warranty type (express, implied, statutory)
Document all defects with evidence references
Draft demand letter with specific cure period
Cite applicable warranty statutes and contract terms
Send via certified mail with return receipt
escalation_path: If contractor fails to cure within demand period, escalate to state licensing board, bond claim, and consider litigation.
human_review_boundary: All demand letters require human attorney review before sending; no automated legal document delivery.
source_basis: Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act (15 USC 2301); state implied warranty of habitability/workmanship statutes; contract law.

purpose: Generate an identity theft affidavit for use with creditors, law enforcement, and credit bureaus. Applies to confirmed or suspected identity theft incidents.
evidence_required_before_use: Police report or FTC Identity Theft Report
List of fraudulent accounts or transactions
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Gather all fraudulent account information
Complete FTC Identity Theft Affidavit form
Attach supporting documentation for each fraudulent item
Generate customized dispute letters for each creditor
Track responses and follow up on unresolved items
escalation_path: If creditors refuse to close fraudulent accounts or continue reporting, escalate to CFPB, state AG, and potential private litigation.
human_review_boundary: Affidavit accuracy and creditor letter customization require human review; template population can be automated.
source_basis: FTC Identity Theft Affidavit procedures; FCRA 15 USC 1681c-2 blocking provisions; state identity theft statutes.

purpose: Generate coordinated notification letters to multiple agencies and institutions for complex cross-domain harms. Applies when a single incident triggers obligations to multiple parties.
evidence_required_before_use: Complete incident documentation across all domains
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify all required notifications and deadlines
Draft letters tailored to each recipient's requirements
Ensure consistent factual statements across all letters
Include required consumer authorization where needed
Track delivery confirmations and response deadlines
escalation_path: If agencies request conflicting actions or impose penalties, escalate to legal counsel specializing in multi-jurisdictional compliance.
human_review_boundary: All multi-agency letters require human legal review; automated deadline tracking and template population only.
source_basis: Federal and state notification requirements by sector; CFPB compliance bulletin on coordinated examinations; state AG coordination protocols.

purpose: Document a credit card billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act including all required elements. Applies to billing errors, unauthorized charges, and goods/services not received.
evidence_required_before_use: Written dispute sent within 60 days of statement
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify disputed transaction(s) with date, amount, merchant
Draft written dispute including all FCBA required elements
Send dispute to creditor's billing inquiry address
Maintain copy of dispute and proof of mailing
Track creditor's 90-day resolution deadline
escalation_path: If creditor fails to respond within 90 days or issues adverse determination without reasonable investigation, escalate to CFPB and consider FCBA action.
human_review_boundary: Dispute letter and evidence compilation require human review; deadline tracking can be automated.
source_basis: FCBA 15 USC 1666; Regulation Z 12 CFR 1026.12-13; CFPB Bulletin on billing error resolution.

purpose: Document an overdraft or NSF fee dispute for submission to a depository institution, emphasizing opt-in status and fee sequencing practices. Applies to checking account overdraft fees.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify consumer's opt-in status under Regulation E
Document all fees with dates, amounts, and triggering transactions
Research institution's transaction ordering practices
Prepare dispute citing relevant regulatory guidance
Submit dispute and track 10 business day investigation period
escalation_path: If institution refuses reversal and fees exceed $100 in 12 months, escalate to CFPB complaint and consider small claims court.
human_review_boundary: Fee analysis and dispute submission require human review; opt-in status verification can be automated.
source_basis: CFPB Bulletin 2013-08 on overdraft practices; Regulation E 12 CFR 1005.17; FDIC FIL-47-2010 on overdraft payment programs.

purpose: Document mortgage servicing errors under RESPA and TILA including payment misapplication, escrow mismanagement, and force-placed insurance. Applies to all federally related mortgage loans.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify specific servicing error type and regulatory basis
Request complete payment history and escrow analysis from servicer
Document all communications with servicer
Submit qualified written request (QWR) under RESPA Section 6
Track 20 business day acknowledgment and 60 day response deadlines
escalation_path: If servicer fails to respond to QWR or error causes material harm, escalate to CFPB, HUD, and consider RESPA private action.
human_review_boundary: QWR preparation and submission require human review; payment history analysis can be automated.
source_basis: RESPA 12 USC 2605; Regulation X 12 CFR 1024; TILA 15 USC 1639; CFPB Mortgage Servicing Rules.

purpose: Document cloud service provider disputes including SLA breaches, unexpected charges, and performance shortfalls. Applies to IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS agreements.
evidence_required_before_use: Cloud service agreement with SLA terms
Performance monitoring data and uptime reports
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify specific SLA metric breach with evidence
Gather uptime/downtime logs from independent monitoring
Document all performance degradation incidents
Calculate credits owed per SLA formula
Submit formal dispute via provider's designated channel
escalation_path: If provider denies valid SLA credit or dispute exceeds $50,000, escalate to arbitration per contract or state court.
human_review_boundary: Dispute submission and credit calculation require human review; uptime monitoring can be automated.
source_basis: Cloud service provider SLA terms; UCC Article 2 for software/services; ABA Model Cloud Computing Contract.

purpose: Document disputes related to home inspection failures, missed defects, or inspector negligence. Applies to pre-purchase inspections and warranty inspections.
evidence_required_before_use: Home purchase contract and inspection contingency
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Compare original and subsequent inspection findings
Document defects that should have been identified
Research inspector's licensing and E&O insurance
Notify inspector's E&O carrier of potential claim
Prepare demand letter for inspection fee refund and damages
escalation_path: If inspector's insurance denies claim or defects exceed policy limits, escalate to state licensing board and consult attorney.
human_review_boundary: All inspection dispute documentation and demand letters require human review; no automation for professional negligence claims.
source_basis: State home inspector licensing laws; ASHI Standards of Practice; state professional negligence statutes.

purpose: Demand performance under a new home builder warranty for structural defects, systems failures, or workmanship issues. Applies to express builder warranties and implied statutory warranties.
evidence_required_before_use: Written notice of defect sent to builder
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Review warranty terms and applicable statutory warranties
Obtain independent professional inspection
Send written demand with specific cure period (typically 30 days)
Document all defects with photographs and expert opinions
If builder fails to cure, file claim with builder's insurance or warranty company
escalation_path: If builder is insolvent or warranty company denies claim, escalate to state home builder recovery fund and construction defect attorney.
human_review_boundary: All demand letters and cure period extensions require human review; warranty term analysis can be automated.
source_basis: State new home warranty statutes; NAHB warranty guidelines; state recovery fund claim procedures.

purpose: Demand warranty performance for vehicle defects under manufacturer warranty or state lemon law. Applies to new and CPO vehicle warranty claims.
evidence_required_before_use: Repair orders for all warranty attempts
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Document all repair attempts with dates and descriptions
Verify defect is covered under warranty terms
Send written demand to manufacturer zone office
If applicable, invoke state lemon law rights with required notices
Track repair attempt count for lemon law thresholds
escalation_path: If manufacturer refuses and vehicle meets lemon law criteria, escalate to state lemon law arbitration program or attorney.
human_review_boundary: Demand letters and lemon law election require human review; repair attempt tracking can be automated.
source_basis: Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act (15 USC 2301); state lemon law statutes; manufacturer warranty terms.

purpose: Demand warranty performance for server, storage, or networking hardware failures. Applies to manufacturer warranties and extended service agreements.
evidence_required_before_use: Hardware error logs and diagnostic output
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run manufacturer diagnostics and capture output
Document failure symptoms and environmental conditions
Verify warranty status and coverage period
Submit warranty claim via manufacturer support portal
If denied, escalate to manufacturer account team with technical evidence
escalation_path: If manufacturer refuses valid warranty claim and hardware is business-critical, escalate to legal and consider replacement vendor.
human_review_boundary: Diagnostic interpretation and escalation decisions require human review; warranty status checks can be automated.
source_basis: UCC Article 2 warranty provisions; manufacturer warranty terms; ISO 9001 quality management standards.

purpose: File warranty or E&O claim against a cybersecurity vendor whose product or service failed to prevent a breach. Applies to firewall, EDR, SIEM, and managed security service failures.
evidence_required_before_use: Cybersecurity vendor contract with SLA/warranty terms
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify specific warranty or SLA provision breached
Document product configuration and rule sets at time of incident
Capture logs showing threat bypassed security controls
Calculate direct damages from breach
Submit claim to vendor with evidence package
escalation_path: If vendor denies claim and damages exceed $100,000, escalate to cyber insurance carrier and litigation counsel.
human_review_boundary: All cybersecurity warranty claims require human review with legal and technical experts; no automation.
source_basis: Cyber insurance policy terms; vendor contract warranty clauses; ISO 27001 control effectiveness requirements.

purpose: Coordinate warranty claims across multiple vendors when a defect spans multiple systems or domains. Applies to smart home systems, integrated security systems, and IoT ecosystems.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Determine which component(s) caused the defect
Identify primary vs secondary warranty obligations
Coordinate timing of claims to avoid cross-denials
Document interactions between systems
Engage integrator if systems were professionally installed
escalation_path: If vendors dispute responsibility, escalate to integrator E&O carrier or consider unified litigation naming all vendors.
human_review_boundary: Multi-vendor warranty coordination always requires human case management; no automation.
source_basis: UCC Article 2; Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act; state consumer protection laws; product liability statutes.

purpose: Verify that required building permits were obtained and inspections passed for construction work. Applies to home improvement, renovation, and new construction projects.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Contact local building department to verify permits on file
Request copies of permit applications and inspection records
Verify permits match scope of work performed
Confirm all required inspections were scheduled and passed
Document any missing permits or failed inspections
escalation_path: If unpermitted work is found, contact local building department for enforcement and notify state licensing board.
human_review_boundary: Enforcement action decisions require human review; permit status lookups can be automated.
source_basis: International Building Code (IBC); local building codes; state contractor licensing requirements.

purpose: Verify electrical work was properly permitted and inspected by a licensed electrician. Applies to home sales, insurance claims, and safety investigations.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Check with local electrical inspector for permits
Verify electrician's license with state board
Request inspection certificates for all electrical work
If no permits found, hire licensed electrician for safety inspection
Document any code violations or safety hazards
escalation_path: If immediate safety hazards exist, contact local building department for emergency inspection and utility to disconnect power if needed.
human_review_boundary: Safety hazard assessment requires human expert review; permit lookups can be automated.
source_basis: National Electrical Code (NEC NFPA 70); state electrical licensing laws; local electrical codes.

purpose: Verify plumbing work was properly permitted and passed inspection. Applies to water damage claims, mold remediation, and home purchases.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify plumbing permit with local building department
Check plumber license status with state board
Request inspection records for pressure tests and final inspection
Compare installed work against approved plans
Document any deviations or code violations
escalation_path: If unpermitted plumbing caused water damage, file claim with plumber's insurance and notify state licensing board.
human_review_boundary: Code violation assessment requires human expert review; permit verification can be automated.
source_basis: International Plumbing Code (IPC); state plumbing codes; manufacturer installation requirements.

purpose: Verify HVAC installation or replacement was properly permitted and inspected. Applies to system failures, energy efficiency disputes, and home sales.
evidence_required_before_use: Property address and HVAC system details
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify HVAC permit with local building department
Confirm load calculations were submitted and approved
Check that final inspection included airflow and refrigerant tests
Verify Energy Star or SEER ratings match installed equipment
Document any discrepancies between permit and installation
escalation_path: If HVAC is undersized or improperly installed causing property damage, file warranty claim and notify state licensing board.
human_review_boundary: Technical assessment of HVAC adequacy requires human expert; permit lookups can be automated.
source_basis: International Mechanical Code (IMC); ACCA Manual J load calculation standards; EPA Section 608 requirements.

purpose: Verify roofing work was properly permitted and inspected. Applies to roof leaks, storm damage claims, and home purchases.
evidence_required_before_use: Roofing contractor license and permit number
Warranty documents for roofing materials and labor
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Confirm roofing permit was obtained for full or partial roof replacement
Verify final inspection was passed
Check that installed materials match permit specifications
Verify manufacturer's installation requirements were followed
Document any permit or inspection discrepancies
escalation_path: If improper installation caused water intrusion and mold, file insurance claim and notify state licensing board of defective work.
human_review_boundary: Water damage and mold assessment require human expert review; permit verification can be automated.
source_basis: International Building Code roofing requirements; manufacturer installation specifications; state roofing contractor licensing.

purpose: Deploy patches for critical CVEs with known exploits in the wild. Applies to all internet-facing systems and critical infrastructure.
evidence_required_before_use: Vendor patch availability and release notes
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify all affected systems in environment
Test patch in non-production environment if possible
Schedule maintenance window for production deployment
Deploy patch with rollback plan prepared
Verify patch installation and re-test for vulnerability
escalation_path: If patch causes system instability or vendor has not released patch, escalate to vendor TAC and implement compensating controls.
human_review_boundary: Production patch deployment timing and rollback decisions require human approval; vulnerability scanning can be automated.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-40 Rev. 4 patch management guide; CISA BOD 22-01 binding operational directive; vendor security advisories.

purpose: Respond to zero-day vulnerabilities when no patch is available. Applies to all systems potentially affected by newly disclosed vulnerabilities.
evidence_required_before_use: Threat intelligence on exploitation in the wild
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Assess vulnerability applicability to environment
Identify compensating controls (WAF rules, IDS signatures)
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable systems
Deploy detection rules for exploitation attempts
Monitor vendor for patch release daily
escalation_path: If exploitation is detected in environment, activate incident response plan and isolate affected systems immediately.
human_review_boundary: Compensating control deployment requires human review; threat intelligence ingestion can be automated.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2 incident response; CISA alerts and advisories; vendor security response center communications.

purpose: Remediate security misconfigurations identified through scanning or audit. Applies to cloud, on-premise, and hybrid infrastructure.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Review scan findings and prioritize by severity
Develop configuration changes to address gaps
Test changes in staging environment
Implement changes during approved maintenance window
Re-scan to verify remediation and document exceptions
escalation_path: If remediation causes application failure or business disruption, escalate to application owner and CAB for exception approval.
human_review_boundary: Configuration changes in production require human approval; scanning and reporting can be automated.
source_basis: CIS Benchmarks; NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 security controls; DISA STIGs; cloud provider security best practices.

purpose: Remediate vulnerabilities in deployed AI models including prompt injection, data poisoning, and adversarial attacks. Applies to all production AI/ML systems.
evidence_required_before_use: Model behavior logs showing anomalous outputs
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Isolate affected model from production traffic
Assess training data for poisoning indicators
Implement input filtering and output sanitization
Re-train or fine-tune model with cleaned data
Deploy updated model with A/B testing and monitoring
escalation_path: If model compromise indicates supply chain attack or insider threat, escalate to security operations and legal.
human_review_boundary: Model retraining and deployment decisions require human review; input/output monitoring can be automated.
source_basis: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Govern and Manage functions; OWASP ML Top 10; MITRE ATLAS framework.

purpose: Remediate gaps in fraud detection and prevention controls after fraud spikes or control failures. Applies to financial institutions, merchants, and fintechs.
evidence_required_before_use: Fraud loss analysis and trend report
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Analyze fraud patterns to identify control weaknesses
Implement additional authentication layers
Update fraud detection rules and machine learning models
Enhance transaction monitoring thresholds
Conduct post-implementation effectiveness testing
escalation_path: If fraud losses exceed risk appetite or regulatory thresholds, escalate to board risk committee and primary regulator.
human_review_boundary: Control design and risk acceptance decisions require human review; fraud detection rule deployment can be automated.
source_basis: FFIEC BSA/AML Examination Manual; NIST SP 800-53 AC and IA controls; ACFE fraud prevention checklists.

purpose: Validate GPU performance against advertised specifications using standardized benchmarks. Applies to AI/ML infrastructure, gaming, and compute-intensive workloads.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run MLPerf inference and training benchmarks
Compare results against published manufacturer scores
Monitor thermal throttling and power consumption
Test with multiple workloads representative of actual use
Document any deviations from specifications
escalation_path: If performance is 10%+ below spec and affects production SLAs, escalate to vendor for replacement or refund.
human_review_boundary: Benchmark methodology and result interpretation require human review; automated benchmark execution is standard.
source_basis: MLPerf benchmark methodology; SPEC gpgpu benchmarks; manufacturer published specifications; ISO 9001 quality standards.

purpose: Validate storage system performance (IOPS, throughput, latency) against specifications. Applies to enterprise storage arrays, NVMe clusters, and cloud storage.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run fio or VDBench with workload profiles matching production
Measure IOPS, throughput, and latency at various queue depths
Compare results against vendor specifications
Test failure scenarios (drive failure, rebuild impact)
Document configuration and any performance anomalies
escalation_path: If storage cannot meet SLA requirements, escalate to vendor for engineering review or initiate SLA credit claim.
human_review_boundary: Benchmark profile selection requires human review; automated benchmark execution and reporting is standard.
source_basis: SNIA performance test specifications; vendor SLA terms; industry-standard benchmark methodologies (fio, VDBench).

purpose: Validate network hardware performance against specifications. Applies to switches, routers, NICs, and load balancers.
evidence_required_before_use: Hardware datasheet with throughput and latency specs
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run RFC 2544 throughput, latency, and frame loss tests
Test with packet sizes representative of production traffic
Measure under stress conditions (multiple flows, multicast)
Verify QoS behavior and prioritization
Document any performance shortfalls
escalation_path: If hardware cannot achieve rated performance in production environment, escalate to vendor for RMA or field engineering visit.
human_review_boundary: Test plan design requires human review; automated RFC 2544 execution is standard.
source_basis: RFC 2544 benchmarking methodology; manufacturer datasheets; IEEE 802.1 standards; vendor test procedures.

purpose: Validate memory subsystem integrity and performance using diagnostic tools. Applies to servers, workstations, and HPC clusters.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run comprehensive memory diagnostic for 24+ hours
Check memory timing against manufacturer specifications
Verify memory population rules per motherboard manual
Test with different memory configurations if errors found
Document all errors with slot locations and patterns
escalation_path: If memory errors persist after replacement, escalate to motherboard/system vendor for hardware review.
human_review_boundary: Error analysis and replacement decisions require human review; automated memory testing is standard.
source_basis: JEDEC memory standards; manufacturer diagnostic procedures; IPMI memory error logging specifications.

purpose: Validate cloud instance performance against advertised instance types. Applies to CPU, memory, network, and storage performance in public cloud.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run sysbench CPU and memory benchmarks
Test network throughput with iperf3 to multiple endpoints
Measure storage IOPS and latency with fio
Compare results against published instance specifications
Run extended tests to detect performance variance/noisy neighbors
escalation_path: If sustained performance is 15%+ below spec, open support case with cloud provider and prepare SLA credit request.
human_review_boundary: SLA credit claim submission requires human review; benchmark execution and monitoring can be fully automated.
source_basis: Cloud provider compute SLAs; sysbench methodology; cloud performance benchmarking best practices.

purpose: Compile documentation to prove the value of hardware assets for insurance claims, tax purposes, or dispute resolution. Applies to data center equipment, HPC clusters, and networking gear.
evidence_required_before_use: Current market pricing for comparable equipment
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Gather all purchase documentation with dates and prices
Research current market prices for equivalent new and used equipment
Run current benchmarks to demonstrate operational capability
Photograph equipment showing condition and serial numbers
Compile depreciation schedule if applicable
escalation_path: If valuation dispute exceeds $50,000 or involves litigation, engage certified equipment appraiser.
human_review_boundary: Final appraisal packet and valuation conclusions require human expert review; document collection can be automated.
source_basis: USPAP appraisal standards; IRS Publication 561; RICS valuation standards; equipment appraiser certification requirements.

purpose: Compile evidence for property damage appraisal including contractor defects, storm damage, or insurance disputes. Applies to residential and commercial property claims.
evidence_required_before_use: Photographs of damage with date stamps
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Obtain independent inspection from licensed professional engineer or home inspector
Get three repair estimates from licensed contractors
Photograph all damage with detailed descriptions
Gather relevant weather data or maintenance records
Compile title and deed documentation
escalation_path: If insurance company and appraiser valuations differ by more than 20%, invoke appraisal clause in policy for umpire process.
human_review_boundary: Appraisal umpire selection and umpire engagement require human attorney review.
source_basis: State insurance appraisal statutes; AICPA valuation standards; policy appraisal clause provisions; state insurance codes.

purpose: Compile documentation to dispute low vehicle valuation in total loss claims. Applies to auto insurance total loss settlements and gap insurance claims.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Document vehicle condition with photographs pre-loss
Research comparable vehicle sales within 100-mile radius
Compile all maintenance and upgrade receipts
Obtain independent dealer appraisal if needed
Submit counter-appraisal to insurance adjuster
escalation_path: If insurance refuses reasonable settlement, escalate to state insurance department and consider invoking policy appraisal clause.
human_review_boundary: Comparable vehicle selection and valuation conclusion require human review; market data collection can be automated.
source_basis: State insurance fair settlement regulations; CCC/Mitchell/NADA valuation methodologies; state consumer protection laws.

purpose: Compile documentation to establish value of digital assets (cryptocurrency, NFTs, domain names) for tax, insurance, or dispute purposes. Applies to estate planning, divorce, and fraud recovery.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Export complete transaction history from all wallets and exchanges
Research market price on relevant dates using CoinMarketCap or exchange data
Document any staking rewards or airdrops received
Compile exchange statements showing cost basis
Engage crypto-savvy CPA or certified appraiser for complex valuations
escalation_path: If valuation exceeds $100,000 or involves disputed ownership, engage attorney with digital asset expertise and certified appraiser.
human_review_boundary: Digital asset valuation methodology requires human expert review; transaction export can be automated.
source_basis: IRS Notice 2014-21 cryptocurrency guidance; AICPA digital asset accounting guidance; state property division statutes.

purpose: Compile documentation to establish software license compliance and value for vendor audits or M&A due diligence. Applies to enterprise software, SaaS, and open-source compliance.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Inventory all software installations across environment
Reconcile installations against license entitlements
Collect usage data for concurrent/user-based licenses
Document any custom terms or enterprise agreements
Calculate effective license position (ELP)
escalation_path: If audit exposure exceeds $100,000 or vendor threatens termination, engage software asset management consultant and legal counsel.
human_review_boundary: ELP calculation and audit response strategy require human expert review; software inventory can be automated.
source_basis: ISO/IEC 19770-1 SAM standard; vendor audit rights clauses; BSA/SIIA audit procedures; contract law.

purpose: Trace the provenance of a wire transfer to identify origin, intermediaries, and destination. Applies to business email compromise, authorized push payment fraud, and wire fraud recovery.
evidence_required_before_use: SWIFT MT103 message or FedWire reference
Email communications related to the transfer
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Obtain full wire trail from originating bank
Trace through correspondent and intermediary banks
Identify beneficiary bank and account
File fraud reports with originating and receiving banks
Request hold/freeze on beneficiary account if fraud confirmed
escalation_path: If funds have moved to foreign jurisdiction or mule accounts, escalate to FBI IC3 and Secret Service immediately.
human_review_boundary: Freeze requests and legal action decisions require human review; wire trace documentation can be automated.
source_basis: UCC Article 4A funds transfer rules; SWIFT messaging standards; FinCEN wire fraud guidance; FBI BEC recovery procedures.

purpose: Trace the provenance of fraudulent checks to identify how they entered the banking system. Applies to counterfeit checks, altered checks, and forged endorsements.
evidence_required_before_use: Image of fraudulent check (front and back)
Deposit trail showing where check was deposited
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Obtain check images from paying bank
Trace endorsement chain and deposit location
Compare signature to authorized specimens
Identify account where check was deposited
File police report and request hold on receiving account
escalation_path: If organized check fraud ring is suspected, escalate to US Postal Inspection Service and FBI Financial Crimes.
human_review_boundary: All check fraud investigations require human review; no automation for legal action decisions.
source_basis: UCC Articles 3 and 4 check liability rules; state check fraud statutes; FBI check fraud investigation protocols.

purpose: Trace cryptocurrency transactions across blockchain to identify destination addresses and potential off-ramps. Applies to exchange hacks, ransomware payments, and investment fraud.
evidence_required_before_use: Blockchain explorer access or analytics tool
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Trace transaction path through blockchain using analytics tools (Chainalysis, TRM, Elliptic)
Identify cluster addresses belonging to exchanges or services
File legal request for KYC data with identified exchanges
Monitor flagged addresses for future activity alerts
Coordinate with law enforcement for seizure orders
escalation_path: If funds moved through mixing service or privacy coin, escalate to FBI cryptocurrency task force and IRS-CI.
human_review_boundary: Legal requests and law enforcement coordination require human review; blockchain tracing can be automated.
source_basis: FinCEN cryptocurrency guidance; blockchain analytics methodologies; federal seizure warrant procedures; exchange subpoena processes.

purpose: Trace ACH debit transactions to identify originators and establish whether authorization existed. Applies to unauthorized ACH debits and revoked authorizations.
evidence_required_before_use: ACH entry details (ODFI, RDFI, trace number, company ID)
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify originating depository financial institution (ODFI)
Research originator using company ID and name
Check for valid authorization on file
If unauthorized or revoked, file ACH return within deadline (60 days for consumer)
Document all communications with ODFI and originator
escalation_path: If ODFI refuses return or originator continues unauthorized debits, escalate to NACHA and CFPB.
human_review_boundary: ACH return decisions require human review; ODFI identification can be automated.
source_basis: NACHA Operating Rules; Regulation E 12 CFR 1005.10; CFPB bulletin on unauthorized ACH transactions.

purpose: Trace gift card transactions to identify redemption points and potential recovery options. Applies to scam victims who purchased gift cards under false pretenses.
evidence_required_before_use: Gift card numbers and purchase receipts
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Contact gift card issuer immediately to report fraud and check balance
Request transaction history for the card
Identify where/when card was redeemed
File report with merchant where cards were purchased
Report to FTC and FBI IC3
escalation_path: If cards were redeemed at known scammer-friendly exchanges, coordinate with issuer fraud team for recovery.
human_review_boundary: Recovery strategy decisions require human review; balance checks can be automated.
source_basis: Gift card terms and conditions; merchant fraud policies; FTC gift card scam consumer alerts; state consumer protection laws.

purpose: Establish and maintain chain of custody for digital evidence to ensure legal admissibility. Applies to forensic investigations, litigation support, and regulatory examinations.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Document evidence acquisition with timestamp and custodian
Generate and record cryptographic hashes immediately upon acquisition
Store evidence in write-once media or WORM storage
Log every access with reason, person, and timestamp
Transfer custody only with signed receipt and hash verification
escalation_path: If chain of custody is broken or questioned, engage forensic expert and notify legal counsel immediately.
human_review_boundary: Custody transfers and access authorizations require human review; hash generation and logging can be automated.
source_basis: ISO/IEC 27037 digital evidence collection; NIST SP 800-86 forensic techniques; FRE 901 authentication requirements.

purpose: Maintain chain of custody for hardware assets during litigation, insurance claims, or recovery. Applies to seized equipment, failed components under warranty, and stolen asset recovery.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Photograph asset condition at transfer
Record serial numbers, asset tags, and configurations
Complete custody transfer form with both party signatures
Store in secure location with access logging
Track asset location at all times until final disposition
escalation_path: If asset is lost, damaged, or tampered with in custody, file incident report and notify insurance and legal.
human_review_boundary: All custody transfers require human documentation and signatures; no automation.
source_basis: ITIL asset management practices; evidence handling procedures; organizational asset disposition policies.

purpose: Maintain proper custody of consumer dispute documents to prevent loss and ensure regulatory compliance. Applies to credit disputes, loan modifications, and foreclosure defense.
evidence_required_before_use: Complete copy of all dispute documents
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Make copies of all original documents before submission
Send all documents via certified mail with return receipt
Log document submission dates and recipient confirmations
Follow up if no acknowledgment within 10 business days
Store originals securely with access logging
escalation_path: If documents are lost by institution or consumer denied opportunity to submit, escalate to regulator and document the loss.
human_review_boundary: Document handling and submission require human review; deadline reminders can be automated.
source_basis: RESPA document retention requirements; FCRA dispute handling requirements; state foreclosure defense document rules.

purpose: Maintain chain of custody for construction material samples used in defect claims and warranty disputes. Applies to defective materials, product liability claims, and code compliance issues.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Collect samples from installed location without contamination
Seal and label with date, location, and collector
Store in climate-controlled environment
Document transfer to testing laboratory with signed receipts
Verify laboratory accreditation and testing methodology
escalation_path: If laboratory results confirm material defect, preserve samples for litigation and notify manufacturer.
human_review_boundary: Sample collection and laboratory selection require human expert involvement; no automation.
source_basis: ASTM material testing standards; ISO/IEC 17025 laboratory accreditation; state product liability statutes.

purpose: Coordinate chain of custody across multiple domains and jurisdictions when evidence spans financial, physical, and digital realms. Applies to complex fraud cases and multi-party disputes.
evidence_required_before_use: Jurisdictional requirements for each evidence type
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Create unified evidence inventory with cross-references
Identify custody requirements for each jurisdiction
Designate primary evidence custodian
Establish secure transfer protocols between domains
Document all access and transfers with legal hold compliance
escalation_path: If evidence must be shared across jurisdictions with conflicting rules, escalate to legal counsel with multi-jurisdictional expertise.
human_review_boundary: Multi-domain evidence custody always requires human legal oversight; no automation.
source_basis: Federal Rules of Evidence; state evidence rules; international evidence sharing agreements (MLAT); organizational legal hold procedures.

purpose: Escalate AI-driven decisions for human review when adverse action is taken or bias is suspected. Applies to credit decisions, hiring, insurance underwriting, and tenant screening.
evidence_required_before_use: AI decision output with confidence score
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify decision as adverse action requiring human review
Document all input features and their values
Check for potentially discriminatory feature usage
Prepare adverse action notice with specific reasons
Route to human reviewer with full context
escalation_path: If bias is confirmed or decision involves protected class, escalate to compliance officer and legal counsel immediately.
human_review_boundary: All adverse AI decisions require mandatory human review; automated identification of adverse actions only.
source_basis: ECOA adverse action requirements; FCRA adverse action notices; NYC Local Law 144 AEDT bias audit requirements; EEOC AI guidance.

purpose: Escalate mortgage servicing decisions for human review when loss mitigation applications are denied or foreclosure proceedings initiated in error. Applies to all federally related mortgage loans.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify denial reasons against application completeness
Check for dual tracking violations (foreclosure during loss mitigation review)
Request specific reasons for denial in writing
Appeal denial within required timeframe with additional documentation
Request escalation to senior review if initial denial appears incorrect
escalation_path: If servicer violates loss mitigation timelines or dual tracking rules, escalate to CFPB, HUD, and foreclosure defense attorney.
human_review_boundary: All loss mitigation appeals and escalation requests require human case review; no automation.
source_basis: CFPB Mortgage Servicing Rules (Regulation X); RESPA loss mitigation requirements; state foreclosure prevention laws.

purpose: Escalate fraud alerts for human review when automated systems flag legitimate transactions or freeze consumer accounts. Applies to transaction monitoring and card fraud detection systems.
evidence_required_before_use: Fraud rule or model that triggered alert
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Review triggered transaction in context of customer history
Attempt customer contact via registered phone number
Compare against known fraud patterns and false positive indicators
Document decision rationale for audit trail
If false positive, release hold and update rules to prevent recurrence
escalation_path: If customer cannot be reached and significant funds are frozen, escalate to supervisor for override decision.
human_review_boundary: Account release decisions require human review; automated alert generation is standard.
source_basis: FFIEC BSA/AML manual on transaction monitoring; CFPB Circular on account access; NACHA rules on account freezes.

purpose: Escalate critical security alerts for human review when automated systems detect potential intrusions or anomalous behavior. Applies to SOC operations and managed security services.
evidence_required_before_use: Security alert with severity and indicators
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Validate alert is not a false positive through initial triage
Assess potential business impact of confirmed threat
Determine if incident response plan should be activated
Engage appropriate technical teams for containment
Document all decisions and actions taken
escalation_path: If threat is confirmed as active intrusion or ransomware deployment, activate full incident response immediately.
human_review_boundary: Incident response activation and containment decisions require human review; automated alert triage is standard.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2 incident handling; SANS SOC escalation procedures; MITRE ATT&CK framework for threat validation.

purpose: Escalate complex cases that span multiple domains for coordinated human review. Applies to cases where single-domain fixers are insufficient.
evidence_required_before_use: Case summary across all affected domains
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Assemble cross-functional review team with domain expertise
Identify interdependencies between domain-specific actions
Develop coordinated response plan
Assign primary owner for case management
Schedule regular sync meetings until resolution
escalation_path: If case involves potential criminal activity, regulatory enforcement, or class action exposure, escalate to general counsel immediately.
human_review_boundary: Cross-domain complex cases always require human case management; no automation.
source_basis: Organizational escalation policies; regulatory examination procedures; legal hold and privilege requirements.

purpose: Refer evidence of systemic consumer financial protection violations to CFPB for supervisory or enforcement action. Applies to pattern or practice violations across multiple consumers.
evidence_required_before_use: Evidence of pattern or practice (multiple consumer complaints)
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Compile complaint data showing pattern or practice
Identify specific regulatory violations
Quantify consumer harm in aggregate
Submit referral to CFPB via complaint portal or whistleblower program
Cooperate with CFPB investigation if initiated
escalation_path: If CFPB does not act within 180 days and violations continue, escalate to congressional oversight committee and state AGs.
human_review_boundary: All regulatory referrals require human expert review and legal consultation; no automation.
source_basis: CFPB Supervision and Examination Manual; CFPB Whistleblower Act procedures; 12 USC 5493 examination authority.

purpose: Refer investment fraud to the Securities and Exchange Commission for enforcement action. Applies to Ponzi schemes, pump and dump, and unregistered securities offerings.
evidence_required_before_use: Investor complaint and financial loss documentation
Broker-dealer or investment adviser CRD number if applicable
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Document fraud scheme structure and participant list
Calculate aggregate investor losses
Identify unregistered securities or unlicensed sellers
File SEC Tips, Complaints, and Referrals (TCR) report
Notify state securities regulators
escalation_path: If scheme is ongoing and causing immediate harm, also contact FBI for criminal investigation and request asset freeze.
human_review_boundary: SEC referrals require human expert review; no automation for securities fraud reporting.
source_basis: Securities Act of 1933; Securities Exchange Act of 1934; SEC Enforcement Manual; state securities (Blue Sky) laws.

fixer_name: State Attorney General Data Breach Referral
purpose: Refer data breaches affecting state residents to the appropriate state attorney general offices. Applies to breaches involving PII, financial data, or health information.
evidence_required_before_use: Breach notification letters sent to affected residents
Number of affected residents per state
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify all states with affected residents
Determine each state's breach notification requirements
Compile breach details and remedial actions taken
Submit notifications/referrals to each affected state AG
Maintain records of all submissions for compliance audit
escalation_path: If breach involves more than 500,000 residents or negligence, expect multi-state AG investigation and prepare legal defense.
human_review_boundary: Breach scope and notification decisions require human legal review; affected resident counts can be automated.
source_basis: State data breach notification laws (all 50 states); NAAG data breach protocols; CCPA/CPRA requirements for California residents.

purpose: Refer housing discrimination complaints to HUD's Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (FHEO). Applies to discrimination in housing sales, rentals, lending, and contractor services.
evidence_required_before_use: Complaint narrative with specific discriminatory acts
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Document all discriminatory statements or actions
Identify protected class basis (race, color, religion, sex, disability, familial status, national origin)
File HUD complaint within one year of violation
Include all evidence and witness contact information
Track 100-day investigation deadline
escalation_path: If HUD issues reasonable cause determination, case proceeds to administrative hearing or federal court; engage attorney.
human_review_boundary: HUD complaint preparation requires human review; deadline tracking can be automated.
source_basis: Fair Housing Act 42 USC 3601; HUD FHEO complaint procedures; state fair housing laws.

purpose: Coordinate referrals to multiple regulators when violations span multiple regulatory frameworks. Applies to fintechs, neobanks, and large financial institutions with multiple regulators.
evidence_required_before_use: Violation analysis across all regulatory frameworks
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Map violations to applicable regulatory schemes
Identify primary vs secondary regulators for each violation
Prepare tailored referral packages for each regulator
Coordinate timing to avoid interference between investigations
Establish communication protocol with all regulators
escalation_path: If regulators initiate parallel investigations, engage specialized counsel for each regulatory relationship.
human_review_boundary: Multi-regulatory referrals always require human legal review; no automation.
source_basis: Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council (FFIEC) coordination; CFPB interagency agreements; state-federal regulatory coordination protocols.

purpose: Educate consumers on credit freezes and facilitate implementation with all four credit bureaus. Applies to identity theft victims and proactive identity protection.
evidence_required_before_use: Current addresses for past two years
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Explain credit freeze vs fraud alert vs credit lock differences
Provide step-by-step freeze instructions for Equifax, Experian, TransUnion, Innovis
Assist with online, phone, or mail freeze requests
Document PINs and confirmation numbers for each bureau
Schedule annual reminder for freeze review
escalation_path: If consumer needs credit access urgently (mortgage application), temporarily lift freeze and re-freeze afterward.
human_review_boundary: Consumer communication requires human interaction; freeze process can be automated via APIs where available.
source_basis: FCRA 15 USC 1681c-1 free credit freeze requirements; state credit freeze laws; CFPB consumer advisory on credit freezes.

purpose: Educate homeowners on recognizing and avoiding loan modification scams and foreclosure rescue fraud. Applies to distressed homeowners seeking loss mitigation.
evidence_required_before_use: State of residence for specific protections
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Explain that no third party can guarantee loan modification
Warn against advance fee demands for modification services
Provide legitimate HUD-approved housing counselor contact information
Explain MARS Rule prohibitions on upfront fees
Document education provided for compliance
escalation_path: If homeowner has already paid fees to scammer, assist with complaint filing to FTC, state AG, and CFPB.
human_review_boundary: Consumer education requires human delivery; scam identification can be partially automated.
source_basis: CFPB Mortgage Assistance Relief Services (MARS) Rule 12 CFR 1015; FTC MARS enforcement; HUD housing counseling program.

purpose: Educate consumers on their rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act and state debt collection laws. Applies to consumers contacted by third-party debt collectors.
evidence_required_before_use: Debt collector's name and contact information
Debt amount and original creditor claimed
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Explain FDCPA rights: validation, limitation on contact times, prohibition on harassment
Provide state-specific debt collection protections
Explain statute of limitations on debt collection
Advise on validation letter strategy
Provide CFPB complaint and state AG contact information
escalation_path: If collector uses threats, harassment, or false statements, document violations and escalate to CFPB and attorney for FDCPA lawsuit.
human_review_boundary: Consumer education requires human delivery; state-specific rights lookup can be automated.
source_basis: FDCPA 15 USC 1692; state debt collection statutes; CFPB debt collection educational materials; state attorney general consumer protection divisions.

purpose: Educate consumers on recognizing common imposter scams including government impersonators, tech support fraud, and family emergency scams. Applies to all consumers, especially elderly and vulnerable populations.
evidence_required_before_use: Scam narrative from consumer or family member
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Explain that government agencies never demand gift cards or wire transfers
Teach verification techniques: hang up and call back on official number
Provide FTC Consumer Sentinel scam reporting information
Discuss common psychological tactics used by scammers
Connect with local adult protective services if elderly victim
escalation_path: If consumer has lost money, assist with recovery efforts and report to appropriate law enforcement.
human_review_boundary: Consumer education requires human delivery; scam pattern matching can be partially automated.
source_basis: FTC Consumer Sentinel Network data; DOJ Elder Justice Initiative; CFPB Office of Older Americans; state adult protective services laws.

purpose: Provide targeted cybersecurity education after security incidents caused by human error. Applies to organizations with employees who clicked phishing links or mishandled data.
evidence_required_before_use: Incident report showing human error factor
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Conduct non-punitive incident debrief with employee
Provide targeted training on incident type (phishing, social engineering)
Review organizational security policies relevant to incident
Assess need for access restriction during retraining
Schedule follow-up phishing simulation to verify learning
escalation_path: If incident involved intentional wrongdoing or repeated negligence after training, escalate to HR and legal.
human_review_boundary: Training delivery can be automated; incident debrief and policy review require human interaction.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-50 security awareness program; SANS security awareness training; organizational acceptable use policies.

purpose: Detect and correct hallucinated or factually incorrect outputs from large language models. Applies to customer-facing chatbots, content generation, and decision support systems.
evidence_required_before_use: Ground truth sources or knowledge base
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Run output through fact-checking against authoritative sources
Check for self-contradictions within the output
Compare against retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) source documents
Flag uncertain claims for human expert verification
Log all corrections for model fine-tuning feedback
escalation_path: If hallucination rate exceeds acceptable threshold or affects safety-critical decisions, remove model from production.
human_review_boundary: Output involving legal, medical, or financial advice requires mandatory human verification; automated pre-screening only.
source_basis: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Measure and Manage functions; OWASP LLM Top 10; IEEE 2857-2021 AI reliability standards.

purpose: Verify outputs from credit scoring models for accuracy, fairness, and regulatory compliance. Applies to FICO, VantageScore, and proprietary scoring models.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify reason codes are accurate and complete (ECOA adverse action requirements)
Check for disparate impact on protected classes
Validate score against known scorecard logic
Document any anomalies or unexpected score variations
Escalate to model risk management if discrepancies found
escalation_path: If model shows bias against protected class or score is inexplicably incorrect, escalate to compliance and legal immediately.
human_review_boundary: All credit scoring disputes require human review; automated reason code validation can be used for pre-screening.
source_basis: ECOA 15 USC 1691; FCRA 15 USC 1681; CFPB Circular 2023-03 on adverse action; SR 11-7 model risk management.

purpose: Verify outputs from computer vision models used for authentication, surveillance, or quality control. Applies to facial recognition, object detection, and medical imaging.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Check confidence score against acceptable threshold
Compare against ensemble model predictions if available
Test for adversarial perturbations on input
For facial recognition, verify with liveness detection
Flag low-confidence predictions for human review
escalation_path: If model is being attacked adversarially or false accept rate exceeds thresholds, disable model and investigate.
human_review_boundary: Biometric authentication failures always require human review; automated confidence scoring can pre-screen.
source_basis: NIST FRVT evaluation standards; ISO/IEC 30107-3 PAD; FDA guidance on AI/ML medical devices.

purpose: Verify outputs from fraud detection models to balance catch rate with false positive rate. Applies to transaction monitoring, account opening, and payment fraud systems.
evidence_required_before_use: Model alert with score and features
Historical performance metrics (catch rate, FP rate)
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Review alert features for coherence and completeness
Check if alert aligns with known fraud patterns
Assess customer impact if transaction is blocked
Compare against recent model performance trends
Escalate if model performance has degraded significantly
escalation_path: If false positive rate exceeds organizational threshold or customer complaints spike, escalate to model owner and risk committee.
human_review_boundary: Blocking decisions require human review; alert scoring and feature verification can be automated.
source_basis: FFIEC BSA/AML examination manual; NIST AI RMF; ACFE fraud detection benchmarking.

purpose: Verify outputs from recommendation systems for appropriateness, fairness, and compliance. Applies to lending recommendations, product recommendations, and content curation.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Check recommendation against prohibited content or product list
Verify recommendation complies with fair lending or consumer protection rules
Assess whether recommendation could cause consumer harm
Check for filter bubble or manipulation indicators
Log recommendation rationale for audit
escalation_path: If recommendations systematically discriminate or cause consumer harm, disable system and escalate to compliance.
human_review_boundary: Recommendations involving regulated products or protected classes require human review; content filtering can be automated.
source_basis: ECOA fair lending requirements; CFPB UDAAP guidance; state consumer protection laws; platform terms of service.

purpose: Implement comprehensive audit logging for all AI agent actions and decisions. Applies to autonomous agents, copilots, and RPA systems.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify all agent action types requiring logging
Design log schema with non-repudiable timestamps
Implement tamper-evident log storage
Ensure logs capture input context, model output, and action taken
Establish log review and alerting procedures
escalation_path: If audit logs reveal unauthorized agent actions or data exfiltration, activate incident response immediately.
human_review_boundary: Log architecture design requires human review; log generation and alerting can be automated.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-92 log management; ISO 27001 logging controls; SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria; EU AI Act logging requirements.

purpose: Implement and review audit logging for privileged user and service account activities. Applies to system administrators, cloud root accounts, and service principals.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Enable comprehensive logging on all privileged accounts
Configure log forwarding to tamper-resistant SIEM
Implement real-time alerting on high-risk activities
Schedule regular privileged access reviews
Correlate logs with change management tickets
escalation_path: If audit reveals unauthorized privilege use or credential compromise, disable account and initiate incident response.
human_review_boundary: Alert generation and log correlation can be fully automated; investigation requires human review.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-53 AC-6 least privilege; CIS Control 4; PCI DSS Requirement 10; C2M2 privileged access management.

purpose: Ensure complete audit trail logging for financial transactions including all modifications and approvals. Applies to payment systems, trading platforms, and lending systems.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Log all transaction events: creation, modification, approval, execution
Include user identity, timestamp, and system context
Implement immutable log storage (WORM or blockchain)
Ensure logs support reconstruction of transaction history
Regular audit trail integrity verification
escalation_path: If audit trail gaps or unauthorized modifications are detected, treat as potential fraud incident and investigate.
human_review_boundary: Log integrity verification can be automated; fraud investigation requires human review.
source_basis: PCI DSS Requirement 10; SOX Section 404 IT controls; MiFID II transaction recording requirements; NIST SP 800-92.

purpose: Implement comprehensive audit logging for all infrastructure changes. Applies to cloud infrastructure, on-premise systems, and network equipment.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Enable change logging on all infrastructure components
Require approval workflow for all changes
Log all changes with before/after state comparison
Alert on unauthorized or out-of-band changes
Regular reconciliation of CMDB against actual state
escalation_path: If unauthorized changes are detected that could affect security or compliance, revert immediately and investigate.
human_review_boundary: Change detection alerting can be fully automated; unauthorized change investigation requires human review.
source_basis: ITIL change management practices; NIST SP 800-53 CM controls; CIS Controls; SOX IT change controls.

purpose: Correlate audit logs across multiple domains to detect sophisticated attacks that span organizational boundaries. Applies to supply chain attacks, business email compromise with fraudulent transfers.
evidence_required_before_use: Audit logs from all affected domains
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Collect normalized logs from all relevant systems
Establish correlation rules linking cross-domain events
Analyze for anomalous patterns spanning domains
Map attack progression across domain boundaries
Generate unified incident timeline
escalation_path: If cross-domain correlation reveals active attack in progress, activate incident response for all affected domains.
human_review_boundary: Correlation rule design requires human expertise; automated log correlation and alerting can be implemented.
source_basis: MITRE ATT&CK cross-technique analytics; NIST SP 800-61 incident correlation; SIEM correlation best practices.

purpose: Isolate systems infected with ransomware to prevent lateral movement and further encryption. Applies to all ransomware incidents from initial detection through containment.
evidence_required_before_use: Confirmed ransomware indicators (encrypted files, ransom note)
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Disconnect infected systems from network (physically or logically)
Preserve ransomware binary and ransom note for forensics
Identify patient zero and initial access vector
Check backup systems for infection before use
Activate incident response team and legal counsel
escalation_path: If ransomware has spread to critical systems or backup infrastructure, activate crisis management team and consider external IR firm.
human_review_boundary: Isolation decisions affecting critical business systems require human approval; initial detection alerts can be automated.
source_basis: CISA Ransomware Guide; NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2; FBI ransomware response guidance; No More Ransom project.

purpose: Execute recovery from ransomware using clean backups without paying ransom. Applies to organizations with viable backup and recovery infrastructure.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify backup integrity before restoration
Rebuild affected systems from known-good images
Restore data from clean backup points
Validate restored systems with antivirus and EDR scans
Monitor for re-infection or backdoor persistence
escalation_path: If clean backups do not exist or are also encrypted, engage forensic experts and evaluate all recovery options including legal ransom payment considerations.
human_review_boundary: Recovery execution can be automated; backup viability assessment and payment decisions require human review.
