source_basis: NIST SP 800-34 contingency planning; CISA ransomware recovery guidance; 3-2-1 backup best practices.

purpose: Assess risks and legal implications of paying ransomware demands. Applies when no viable recovery path exists and business continuity is threatened.
evidence_required_before_use: Ransom demand amount and cryptocurrency address
OFAC sanctions screening of ransomware group
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Screen ransomware group against OFAC sanctions list
Assess likelihood of decryption key working
Evaluate legal risks of payment (sanctions, money laundering)
Engage law enforcement and external counsel
Document decision rationale for regulatory defense
escalation_path: If OFAC sanctions are implicated, do not pay; engage FBI and CISA for alternative recovery support.
human_review_boundary: Ransom payment decisions always require human review with legal counsel, C-suite, and board input; no automation.
source_basis: OFAC ransomware advisories; FinCEN ransomware guidance; DOJ ransomware enforcement policy; state ransomware payment notification laws.

purpose: Conduct forensic investigation of ransomware incident to determine root cause, attack timeline, and data exfiltration scope. Applies to all ransomware incidents requiring insurance or regulatory reporting.
evidence_required_before_use: Forensic disk images of affected systems
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Preserve forensic evidence with chain of custody
Analyze ransomware variant and TTPs
Reconstruct attack timeline from initial access to detection
Identify data exfiltration scope from logs
Prepare forensic report for insurance and regulators
escalation_path: If investigation reveals nation-state attribution or supply chain compromise, escalate to CISA, FBI, and national cyber security center.
human_review_boundary: Forensic investigation requires certified human experts; no automation for evidence analysis.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-86 forensic guide; CISA forensic analysis procedures; Chain of custody legal requirements; cyber insurance claim procedures.

purpose: Assess ransomware impact across multiple domains when incident affects interconnected systems. Applies to ransomware affecting OT/IT convergence, supply chain partners, or multi-entity environments.
evidence_required_before_use: Impact assessment across all affected domains
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Map all affected systems across IT, OT, and partner domains
Assess cascading effects on dependent systems
Identify regulatory notification requirements per domain
Coordinate with supply chain partners on shared exposure
Develop unified recovery timeline across domains
escalation_path: If ransomware affected safety-critical OT systems or critical infrastructure, report to CISA and sector ISAC immediately.
human_review_boundary: Cross-domain impact assessment requires human expert coordination; no automation.
source_basis: CISA critical infrastructure guidance; NERC CIP standards for energy sector; sector-specific ISAC protocols; supply chain risk management frameworks.

purpose: Implement security freezes with all four nationwide consumer reporting agencies. Applies to identity theft victims and consumers seeking proactive protection.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Register freeze online with Equifax, Experian, TransUnion, Innovis
Record and securely store all PINs
Confirm freeze is active by attempting soft inquiry
Set calendar reminders to renew or manage freeze
Provide consumer with freeze lift/temporary lift instructions
escalation_path: If freeze request is denied or consumer cannot authenticate, escalate to CFPB and state attorney general.
human_review_boundary: Consumer communication requires human interaction; freeze API calls can be automated.
source_basis: FCRA 15 USC 1681c-1; state credit freeze laws; CFPB freeze implementation guidance.

purpose: Place extended fraud alerts (7 years) with credit bureaus for confirmed identity theft victims. Applies to consumers with FTC Identity Theft Report or police report.
evidence_required_before_use: FTC Identity Theft Report or police report
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Submit identity theft report to all four credit bureaus
Request extended fraud alert (7 years) placement
Request free credit reports to review for additional fraud
Opt out of pre-approved credit offers (5 years or permanent)
Document confirmation numbers and effective dates
escalation_path: If additional fraudulent accounts are discovered during review, initiate FCRA blocking process and file additional police reports.
human_review_boundary: Identity theft counseling requires human interaction; alert placement can be automated via APIs.
source_basis: FCRA 15 USC 1681c-1 extended fraud alerts; FTC Identity Theft Report procedures; CFPB identity theft repair guidance.

purpose: Implement credit lock services as an alternative to credit freezes for consumers prioritizing convenience. Applies to consumers who need frequent credit access flexibility.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Explain differences between freeze (free, legally mandated) and lock (may have fee, contractual)
If consumer chooses lock, enroll in bureau lock services
Configure lock/unlock via mobile app or web
Set up biometric or two-factor authentication for lock control
Provide consumer with quick-lock/quick-unlock instructions
escalation_path: If lock service has technical failures or unauthorized unlock occurs, file CFPB complaint and switch to legally mandated freeze.
human_review_boundary: Consumer education requires human interaction; lock service enrollment can be automated.
source_basis: FCRA credit freeze provisions; bureau lock service terms; CFPB advisory on freeze vs lock differences; state consumer protection laws.

purpose: Establish credit freezes for minor children to prevent synthetic identity fraud. Applies to parents and guardians of children under 18.
evidence_required_before_use: Parent/guardian identification and proof of authority
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Check if child has existing credit file (should not exist for minors)
If credit file exists, request report and document fraudulent accounts
If no file exists, request protective freeze for minor
Submit documentation proving parent/guardian authority
Monitor annually until child turns 18
escalation_path: If child has existing credit file with fraudulent accounts, initiate FCRA blocking process for minors and file identity theft report.
human_review_boundary: Minor identity theft response requires human case management; freeze requests can be automated via APIs.
source_basis: FCRA 15 USC 1681c-1 minor credit freeze provisions; state minor credit protection laws; FTC child identity theft guidance.

purpose: Enroll affected consumers in credit monitoring services following a data breach. Applies to organizations offering breach remediation and affected individuals.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify monitoring service coverage (credit reports, scores, dark web monitoring)
Enroll using provided activation code or reimbursement process
Set up monitoring alerts for all credit bureaus
Review initial credit report for existing fraud
Document enrollment confirmation and service duration
escalation_path: If monitoring reveals existing fraud or new fraudulent accounts appear, activate identity theft restoration services.
human_review_boundary: Consumer counseling requires human interaction; monitoring enrollment via web portals can be automated.
source_basis: State data breach notification laws requiring monitoring; CFPB credit monitoring guidance; state AG breach settlement requirements.

purpose: Verify contractor license, bond, and insurance status before hiring. Applies to all home improvement, repair, and construction projects.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify license is active with state licensing board
Check for disciplinary actions or complaints
Verify contractor bond status and amount
Request and verify certificate of insurance (general liability, workers comp)
Check Better Business Bureau and online reviews
escalation_path: If contractor is unlicensed or uninsured, do not hire; report to state licensing board if they solicited work.
human_review_boundary: License verification can be automated; insurance verification and hiring decisions require human review.
source_basis: State contractor licensing laws; NASCLA verification standards; CSLB (California) model; AARP fraud prevention guidance.

purpose: File a claim against a contractor's surety bond for financial losses from incomplete or defective work. Applies when contractor is unresponsive or insolvent.
evidence_required_before_use: Contractor bond information (carrier, bond number, amount)
Contract and scope of work documentation
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify bond is still active with surety company
Document all incomplete or defective work with evidence
Obtain repair estimates from licensed contractors to complete work
Complete bond claim form with all supporting documentation
Submit claim within bond claim filing deadline
escalation_path: If bond is insufficient to cover losses or surety denies valid claim, escalate to state recovery fund and litigation.
human_review_boundary: Bond claim filing requires human review and preparation; bond status verification can be automated.
source_basis: State contractor bond claim procedures; surety bond claim regulations; state contractor recovery fund statutes; NASCLA guidelines.

purpose: Verify that a contractor maintains adequate insurance coverage before work begins. Applies to projects where property damage or worker injury could occur.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Request COI directly from insurance agent (not contractor)
Verify general liability coverage amount is adequate for project value
Verify workers compensation coverage if contractor has employees
Check if policy includes completed operations coverage
Verify COI names property owner as additional insured if appropriate
escalation_path: If insurance is inadequate or expired, require contractor to obtain proper coverage before work begins or select different contractor.
human_review_boundary: Insurance adequacy assessment requires human review; COI verification can be partially automated.
source_basis: State contractor insurance requirements; risk management best practices; ISO general liability policy forms.

fixer_name: Contractor Reference and Work History Verification
purpose: Verify contractor's work history and references before engagement. Applies to significant projects where contractor experience is critical.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Contact at least three recent references for similar projects
Visit completed projects if possible to inspect quality
Verify permit history on past projects matches contractor claims
Check for liens filed against past projects
Research contractor's business history and any name changes
escalation_path: If references cannot be verified or past projects show permit/lien issues, select different contractor.
human_review_boundary: Reference checks require human interaction; permit history lookups can be automated.
source_basis: BBB accreditation standards; state contractor licensing requirements; consumer protection best practices; construction industry guidelines.

purpose: Verify contractor licensing when work spans multiple jurisdictions. Applies to large projects, federal contracts, and contractors working near state borders.
evidence_required_before_use: All jurisdictions where work will be performed
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify contractor holds valid license in each jurisdiction
Check for interstate reciprocity agreements
Verify local business licenses and permits
Confirm adequate insurance covers all jurisdictions
Check for disciplinary actions in any jurisdiction
escalation_path: If contractor is not properly licensed in a required jurisdiction, work must not proceed until proper licensing is obtained.
human_review_boundary: Multi-jurisdictional verification requires human coordination; individual state lookups can be automated.
source_basis: NASCLA interstate licensing compacts; state reciprocity agreements; local business licensing requirements.

purpose: Implement monitoring for account takeover indicators across consumer and business accounts. Applies to financial institutions, fintechs, and high-value service providers.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Establish baseline account behavior (login times, locations, devices)
Configure alerts for anomalous access patterns
Monitor for credential stuffing attacks via failed login analysis
Check user credentials against dark web breach databases
Implement step-up authentication for high-risk access attempts
escalation_path: If account takeover is confirmed, lock account immediately and initiate recovery protocol.
human_review_boundary: Automated monitoring and alerting is standard; account lock and recovery decisions require human review.
source_basis: FFIEC authentication guidance; NIST SP 800-63B digital identity; CISA credential stuffing guidance; industry ATO prevention best practices.

purpose: Implement real-time transaction monitoring for anomalous patterns indicative of fraud. Applies to payment processors, card issuers, and banks.
evidence_required_before_use: Fraud rule set and model thresholds
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Profile normal transaction patterns (amount, merchant category, geography, velocity)
Set dynamic thresholds based on account history and risk level
Monitor for velocity attacks and card testing
Implement real-time scoring with declined/approved decision
Queue suspicious transactions for manual review
escalation_path: If organized fraud attack is detected (coordinated card testing), activate fraud attack response protocol.
human_review_boundary: Real-time scoring can be fully automated; manual review queue requires human analysts.
source_basis: FFIEC BSA/AML examination manual; NACHA ACH fraud detection guidance; EMVCo fraud monitoring standards; PCI DSS fraud requirements.

purpose: Detect synthetic identity fraud by monitoring for patterns associated with manufactured identities. Applies to account opening, credit underwriting, and portfolio monitoring.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Monitor for credit file thinness with rapid account expansion
Check for PII elements (SSN, DOB, address) that don't correlate
Flag applicants with no established history prior to recent credit building
Monitor for bust-out patterns (high utilization followed by default)
Cross-reference with industry fraud databases
escalation_path: If synthetic identity is confirmed, file SAR, close account, and report to credit bureaus and law enforcement.
human_review_boundary: Synthetic identity confirmation requires human expert review; automated pattern detection can flag suspects.
source_basis: CFPB synthetic identity fraud research; FFIEC fraud examination procedures; ID Analytics synthetic fraud models; credit bureau fraud tools.

purpose: Monitor for business email compromise indicators including spoofed emails, payment redirection, and executive impersonation. Applies to accounts payable, treasury, and procurement functions.
evidence_required_before_use: Vendor master file with verified contacts
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Implement email authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC)
Monitor for lookalike domain registrations
Flag payment instruction changes for verification
Require voice verification for payment changes over threshold
Monitor for anomalous email patterns (timing, tone, urgency)
escalation_path: If BEC attack is confirmed and funds transferred, immediately contact receiving bank and file FBI IC3 report.
human_review_boundary: Automated email analysis and alerting is standard; payment change verification requires human confirmation.
source_basis: FBI BEC threat reports; CISA email security guidance; DMARC.org best practices; treasury management security protocols.

purpose: Monitor for fraud patterns that exploit gaps between channels (online, phone, branch, mobile). Applies to omnichannel financial services and retail.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Correlate activity across all customer channels
Flag channel switches that bypass security controls
Monitor for account changes via one channel followed by transactions via another
Share fraud indicators across channel silos in real-time
Implement channel-aware risk scoring
escalation_path: If cross-channel fraud attack is confirmed, activate unified incident response across all channels and customer touchpoints.
human_review_boundary: Cross-channel correlation can be automated; incident response activation requires human decision.
source_basis: FFIEC omnichannel risk management; NIST cybersecurity framework; PCI DSS multi-channel security; industry fraud sharing consortiums.

purpose: Request reversal of overdraft fees from depository institutions, emphasizing consumer-friendly practices and regulatory expectations. Applies to checking account overdraft fees assessed on debit card transactions and checks.
evidence_required_before_use: Account history showing prior fee patterns
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Review fee assessment for regulatory compliance (Reg E opt-in)
Document the sequence of transactions that triggered fees
Calculate fees as percentage of transaction amounts
Submit written reversal request citing institution's own policies
If denied, escalate to branch manager and document all conversations
escalation_path: If institution refuses and fees appear unfair, file CFPB complaint and consider small claims court action.
human_review_boundary: Reversal request preparation requires human review; fee calculation and pattern analysis can be automated.
source_basis: CFPB Bulletin 2013-08 on overdraft practices; Regulation E 12 CFR 1005.17; FDIC FIL on overdraft payment programs; CFPB overdraft fee guidance.

purpose: Request reversal of multiple NSF fees resulting from the same transaction being represented multiple times. Applies when merchants re-submit checks or ACH debits causing repeated NSF fees.
evidence_required_before_use: Account statement showing repeated NSF fees for same transaction
Consumer balance history showing available funds
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Identify all NSF fees associated with the same underlying transaction
Document each presentment date and fee assessment
Check if institution policies limit fees for same transaction
Submit dispute citing repeated presentment fee limitations
Request refund of fees beyond first NSF for same item
escalation_path: If institution charges more than 4 NSF fees for same transaction or refuses reasonable reversal, escalate to CFPB and state regulator.
human_review_boundary: Fee cascade analysis can be automated; dispute submission requires human review.
source_basis: NACHA rules on ACH representment; UCC Article 4 presentment rules; CFPB bulletin on repeated presentment fees; state UDAP laws.

purpose: Challenge overdraft fees assessed on ATM and debit card transactions when consumer did not opt in or opt-in status is disputed. Applies to Regulation E covered transactions.
evidence_required_before_use: All overdraft fees assessed on ATM/debit transactions
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Verify consumer's opt-in status under Regulation E
Review opt-in notice for compliance with regulatory format requirements
Identify all fees on ATM/debit transactions since opt-in status date
If opt-in was not obtained, demand refund of all non-check overdraft fees
If opt-in notice was non-compliant, argue opt-in is invalid
escalation_path: If institution cannot produce compliant opt-in record, file CFPB complaint and demand full refund of post-2010 opt-in rule overdraft fees on debit transactions.
human_review_boundary: Opt-in status analysis requires human review; fee identification can be automated.
source_basis: Regulation E 12 CFR 1005.17 opt-in requirements; CFPB Examination Manual on overdraft services; CFPB consent orders on opt-in violations.

purpose: Convert from overdraft fee program to overdraft line of credit or linked account transfer to reduce costs. Applies to consumers frequently incurring overdraft fees.
evidence_required_before_use: Qualifying credit profile for line of credit
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Calculate total overdraft fees paid in past 12 months
Compare overdraft LOC APR to per-item overdraft fees
Apply for overdraft line of credit if eligible
Alternatively, link savings account for overdraft transfers
Opt out of overdraft coverage for debit/ATM transactions
escalation_path: If institution denies LOC or transfer service without reasonable basis, file complaint and consider changing institutions.
human_review_boundary: Product comparison can be automated; application and eligibility review require human interaction.
source_basis: CFPB overdraft program analysis; OCC guidance on overdraft LOC products; Regulation E opt-out rights; FCBA linked account protections.

purpose: Assert Military Lending Act protections for active-duty servicemembers regarding overdraft fees and deposit advance products. Applies to covered borrowers under MLA.
evidence_required_before_use: Military orders showing active duty status
Account showing overdraft fees or deposit advance charges
Loan or fee assessment date during covered period
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Confirm MLA coverage period (active duty + 30 months for post-activation)
Calculate Military APR (MAPR) including all fees on covered products
If MAPR exceeds 36%, demand refund of excess charges
Assert MLA protections against mandatory arbitration clauses
File complaint with CFPB and JAG if violations confirmed
escalation_path: If MLA violations are confirmed, servicemember may have private right of action for damages plus attorney fees; engage military legal assistance.
human_review_boundary: MAPR calculation can be automated; legal action decisions require human attorney review.
source_basis: MLA 32 CFR 232; DoD MLA implementation guidance; CFPB MLA examination procedures; JAG legal assistance program.

purpose: Coordinate response to attacks that simultaneously target multiple domains such as ransomware deployment combined with wire fraud and data exfiltration. Applies to sophisticated cybercrime campaigns.
evidence_required_before_use: Incident timeline showing coordinated attack vectors
Affected systems inventory across all domains
Confirmed or suspected threat actor TTPs
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Activate incident response for all affected domains simultaneously
Coordinate containment to prevent attackers from pivoting
Preserve forensic evidence from all attack vectors
Notify law enforcement of multi-vector nature for unified investigation
Communicate with cyber insurance carrier covering all affected areas
escalation_path: If attack involves physical threats, nation-state actors, or critical infrastructure, activate national response framework.
human_review_boundary: Multi-vector coordinated response always requires human expert leadership; no automation.
source_basis: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; CISA incident response guidance; FBI cyber task force coordination protocols; cyber insurance claim procedures.

purpose: Prepare organizations for regulatory examinations across multiple compliance domains. Applies to banks, fintechs, and licensed contractors facing routine or targeted examinations.
evidence_required_before_use: Prior examination findings and remediation status
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Assemble examination response team with domain experts
Gather all requested documents and organize by examination item
Review prior findings for complete remediation evidence
Prepare management responses for known gaps
Schedule mock examination walkthrough
escalation_path: If examination reveals significant compliance breakdowns, engage specialized regulatory counsel immediately.
human_review_boundary: Examination preparation always requires human expert management; document collection can be partially automated.
source_basis: FFIEC examination procedures; CFPB supervision and examination manual; state examination protocols; regulatory counsel engagement procedures.

purpose: Orchestrate comprehensive remediation for consumers harmed across multiple domains. Applies when a single event (data breach, natural disaster, fraud scheme) causes cascading consumer harms.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Assess all domains of harm (financial, physical, digital, emotional)
Identify all available remediation paths
Prioritize remediation by urgency and consumer impact
Coordinate timing of remediation actions
Monitor for secondary harms during remediation process
escalation_path: If remediation involves class action settlement or regulatory restitution program, engage legal counsel for claims filing.
human_review_boundary: Remediation orchestration always requires human case management; no automation.
source_basis: CFPB restitution procedures; class action settlement administration; state consumer restitution funds; victim compensation programs.

purpose: Remediate AI agents that exhibit anomalous behavior due to model drift, prompt injection, or environmental changes. Applies to all production AI agent systems.
evidence_required_before_use: Environmental change log (model updates, data changes)
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Compare current behavior against established baseline
Identify drift indicators (output distribution changes, error rate spikes)
Determine root cause (data drift, concept drift, adversarial input)
Roll back to last known good model version if needed
Implement enhanced monitoring before re-deployment
escalation_path: If agent behavior poses safety risk or affects regulated decisions, remove from production immediately and notify compliance.
human_review_boundary: Agent behavior assessment and rollback decisions require human review; automated drift detection is standard.
source_basis: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Monitor and Manage functions; MLOps best practices; IEEE 2857-2021 AI reliability; Google SRE model serving practices.

purpose: Guide comprehensive recovery after major incidents affecting multiple domains. Applies post-breach, post-fraud campaign, or post-natural disaster affecting consumers and organizations.
steps_at_a_safe_high_level: Validate all systems are clean and secure before restoration
Implement additional controls to prevent recurrence
Notify and educate affected consumers on protective measures
Monitor for delayed effects or secondary attacks
Conduct post-incident review and update response plans
escalation_path: If recovery reveals additional undiscovered compromises, re-activate incident response.
human_review_boundary: Post-incident review and plan updates require human leadership; system validation can be automated.
source_basis: NIST SP 800-61 post-incident activity; ISO 27035 incident management; business continuity planning standards; CISA recovery guidance.
